a11y.trans.1.name: إبعاد
a11y.trans.2.name: عرض الفضاء

a11y.trans.5.name: خروج من النظام

a11y.trans.7.name: الرجوع في الزمن
astrology.element.0.name: النار
astrology.element.1.name: الأرض
astrology.element.2.name: الهواء
astrology.element.3.name: الماء
astrology.modality.0.name: أساسي
astrology.modality.1.name: ثابت
astrology.modality.2.name: متحوّل
astrology.sign.0.brief: الشرارة التي تبدأ — الشجاعة الخام للوجود
astrology.sign.0.name: الحمل
astrology.sign.1.brief: الأرض التي تصمد — الزراعة الصبورة للجمال
astrology.sign.1.name: الثور
astrology.sign.10.brief: الموجة التي تحمل — الحرية في خدمة الجميع
astrology.sign.10.name: الدلو
astrology.sign.11.brief: المحيط الذي يذيب — الوحدة ما وراء الانفصال
astrology.sign.11.name: الحوت
astrology.sign.2.brief: النَّفَس بين الأشياء — التواصل عبر اللغة
astrology.sign.2.name: الجوزاء
astrology.sign.3.brief: الصدفة التي تحمي — الشعور كشكل من المعرفة
astrology.sign.3.name: السرطان
astrology.sign.4.name: الأسد
astrology.sign.5.brief: اليد التي ترمّم — الكمال من خلال التمييز
astrology.sign.5.name: العذراء
astrology.sign.6.name: الميزان
astrology.sign.7.name: العقرب
astrology.sign.8.brief: السهم الذي يسعى — المعنى ما وراء الأفق
astrology.sign.8.name: القوس
astrology.sign.9.brief: الجبل الذي يصمد — السلطة المكتسبة عبر الزمن
astrology.sign.9.name: الجدي

astronomy.phase.0.brief: البذرة المظلمة — خفية، مليئة بالإمكانات، كل شيء يبدأ من هنا
astronomy.phase.0.guidance: ضع النوايا، ابدأ المشاريع، استرح وتأمّل في الظلام
astronomy.phase.0.name: المحاق
astronomy.phase.0.theme: البدايات، زرع البذور، تحديد النوايا، عتمة التجدد
astronomy.phase.1.brief: أول شريحة ضوء — النية تصبح مرئية، النور الفتي يجمع قوته
astronomy.phase.1.guidance: اتخذ الخطوة الأولى، اجمع الموارد، تجاوز المقاومة الأولية
astronomy.phase.1.name: الهلال المتنامي
astronomy.phase.1.theme: النية الناشئة، الالتزام الأول، شجاعة البداية
astronomy.phase.2.brief: الاختيار نصف المضاء — النور والظلام بقدر متساوٍ
astronomy.phase.2.guidance: اتخذ القرارات، تغلّب على العقبات، التزم بالاتجاه المختار
astronomy.phase.2.name: التربيع الأول
astronomy.phase.3.brief: شبه مكتمل — الإعداد الأخير، الصقل قبل الذروة
astronomy.phase.3.guidance: صقّل الخطة، عدّل بناءً على الملاحظات، ثق بالعملية
astronomy.phase.3.name: الأحدب المتنامي
astronomy.phase.4.brief: النور الكامل — كل شيء مُنار، لا شيء مخفي، الذروة
astronomy.phase.4.guidance: احتفل بالإنجاز، انظر بوضوح، احصد ما زُرع عند المحاق
astronomy.phase.4.name: البدر
astronomy.phase.5.brief: التراجع الكريم — النور يبدأ في إهداء ذاته
astronomy.phase.5.guidance: شارك ما تعلّمته، أعطِ، عبّر عن الامتنان
astronomy.phase.5.name: الأحدب المتناقص
astronomy.phase.6.brief: التحرّر نصف المضاء — النور والظلام متساويان لكن النور يرحل
astronomy.phase.6.guidance: تخلَّ عمّا لم يعد يخدمك، سامح، أفسح المجال للجديد
astronomy.phase.6.name: التربيع الأخير
astronomy.phase.7.brief: الهمسة الأخيرة — أرقّ نور قبل العتمة، حكمة الاستسلام
astronomy.phase.7.guidance: استرح عميقاً، استسلم، استعدّ للظلام القادم
astronomy.phase.7.name: الهلال المتناقص
astronomy.phase.7.theme: الاستسلام، الراحة، الاستعداد للفراغ، المرحلة البلسمية
astronomy.planet.0.archetype: المحتال-الرسول — عقل زئبقي، مرافق الأرواح بين العوالم
astronomy.planet.0.name: عطارد
astronomy.planet.1.archetype: المحبوبة — مرآة الرغبة، الجمال كقوة حضارية
astronomy.planet.1.name: الزهرة
astronomy.planet.2.archetype: الأم — العالم الحي الذي يدعم كل مراقبة للكون
astronomy.planet.2.brief: النقطة الزرقاء الباهتة — الكوكب الذي يعيد النظر إلى السماء
astronomy.planet.2.name: الأرض
astronomy.planet.3.archetype: المحارب — القوة الحمراء التي تقاتل من أجل ما يهم
astronomy.planet.3.brief: الكوكب الأحمر — صدئ الحديد، بلون الدم
astronomy.planet.3.name: المريخ
astronomy.planet.4.archetype: الملك — أكبر جرم بعد الشمس، الحامي والحاكم
astronomy.planet.4.brief: المنعم الأكبر — أكبر كتلة من الكواكب مجتمعة
astronomy.planet.4.name: المشتري
astronomy.planet.5.archetype: الشيخ — الحدّ المحلّق، الدرس الذي لا مفرّ منه
astronomy.planet.5.brief: المعلّم المحلّق — جميل وصارم، سيد الزمن
astronomy.planet.5.name: زحل
astronomy.planet.6.brief: المتمرّد المائل — كوكب يكسر كل قاعدة
astronomy.planet.6.name: أورانوس
astronomy.planet.7.archetype: المتصوّف — المحيط الذائب حيث تنتهي الحدود
astronomy.planet.7.name: نبتون
astronomy.tpl.detail_retro: تراجع: حركة ظاهرية عكسية — مواضيع الكوكب تتّجه للداخل للمراجعة.
astronomy.tpl.detail_retrograde: تراجع: حركة ظاهرية عكسية — مواضيع الكوكب تتّجه للداخل للمراجعة.
aztec.bearer.0.brief: Rabbit years — the south wind brings abundance and the danger of excess
aztec.bearer.0.quality: Abundance, fertility, lunar influence — a year of growth and celebration
aztec.bearer.1.brief: Reed years — the east wind brings vision and the burden of power
aztec.bearer.1.quality: Authority, knowledge, beginnings — a year of leadership and destiny
aztec.bearer.2.brief: Flint years — the north wind brings clarity through the cold blade of truth
aztec.bearer.2.quality: Sacrifice, truth, hardship — a year of testing and purification
aztec.bearer.3.brief: House years — the west wind brings the comfort of home and the labor of building
aztec.bearer.3.quality: Rest, family, introspection — a year of shelter and inner work
aztec.sign.0.brief: The crocodile — first sign, the earth monster from whose body the world was made
aztec.sign.0.quality: Beginnings, primal energy, the creative surge
aztec.sign.1.brief: The wind — the feathered serpent's breath that sweeps across creation
aztec.sign.10.brief: The monkey — the flower prince, patron of art, music, and sacred games
aztec.sign.10.quality: Play, art, dance, the creative spirit unchained
aztec.sign.11.brief: The grass — twisted fiber that binds and heals, the herbalist's sign
aztec.sign.11.quality: Tenacity, endurance, the medicine that heals and harms
aztec.sign.12.brief: The reed — the smoking mirror, knowledge that cuts like an obsidian blade
aztec.sign.12.quality: Authority, knowledge, the arrow of truth, hollow vessel for spirit
aztec.sign.13.brief: The jaguar — the earth goddess who devours sin and births warriors
aztec.sign.13.quality: Courage, the warrior's heart, night vision, hidden power
aztec.sign.14.brief: The eagle — the flayed god of spring, the sun's warrior who rises reborn
aztec.sign.14.quality: Vision, freedom, the sun warrior, ascent to heaven
aztec.sign.15.brief: The vulture — the obsidian butterfly, elder wisdom that transforms death into life
aztec.sign.15.quality: Wisdom of age, patience, purification through decay
aztec.sign.16.brief: Earthquake — the dog-headed twin who carried the sun through the underworld
aztec.sign.16.quality: Movement, change, the heartbeat of the Fifth Sun
aztec.sign.17.brief: The flint knife — the jeweled fowl, obsidian that reveals by dividing
aztec.sign.17.quality: Sacrifice, clarity, the cut that separates truth from illusion
aztec.sign.18.brief: Rain — she who dwells in the house, the hearth fire and the storm
aztec.sign.18.quality: Nourishment, cleansing, the storm that feeds and destroys
aztec.sign.19.brief: The flower — the quetzal-feathered goddess of love, art, and sacred beauty
aztec.sign.19.quality: Beauty, pleasure, art, the flowering of consciousness
aztec.sign.2.brief: The house — the heart of the mountain, protection and introspection
aztec.sign.2.quality: Shelter, family, rest, the inner world
aztec.sign.3.brief: The lizard — quick, cunning, surviving by wits in the sun
aztec.sign.4.brief: The serpent — jade-skirted goddess of flowing water and hidden knowledge
aztec.sign.4.quality: Power, sensuality, wisdom coiled in the body
aztec.sign.5.brief: Death — the moon god who sacrificed himself and was reborn as light
aztec.sign.5.quality: Transformation, release, the gateway between worlds
aztec.sign.6.brief: The deer — gentle creature of the rain god, grace in the wild
aztec.sign.6.quality: Grace, gentleness, the forest's gift, sensitivity
aztec.sign.7.brief: The rabbit — the maguey goddess, four hundred rabbits of the moon
aztec.sign.7.quality: Fertility, abundance, intoxication, the lunar hare
aztec.sign.8.brief: Water — the turquoise lord of fire who rules through the paradox of opposites
aztec.sign.8.quality: Purification, emotion, the destructive and nurturing flood
aztec.sign.9.brief: The dog — guide of the dead across the nine rivers of Mictlan
aztec.sign.9.quality: Loyalty, guidance to the underworld, the soul's companion
bahai.holy_day.1.brief: The Baha'i New Year — the day the Sun enters Aries, ending the Fast
bahai.holy_day.1.name: Naw-Ruz
bahai.holy_day.1.theme: New Year — 1 Baha, the vernal equinox, rebirth and renewal
bahai.holy_day.2.brief: The holiest day — when Baha'u'llah announced His mission in the garden of paradise
bahai.holy_day.2.name: First Day of Ridvan
bahai.holy_day.2.theme: Declaration of Baha'u'llah in the Garden of Ridvan — the Most Great Festival
bahai.holy_day.3.brief: The ninth day of Ridvan — reunion in the garden of declaration
bahai.holy_day.3.name: Ninth Day of Ridvan
bahai.holy_day.3.theme: Family of Baha'u'llah joins Him in the garden
bahai.holy_day.4.brief: The twelfth and final day of Ridvan — the 12-day festival concludes
bahai.holy_day.4.name: Twelfth Day of Ridvan
bahai.holy_day.4.theme: Departure from the garden — conclusion of the Most Great Festival
bahai.holy_day.5.brief: The Bab's declaration — the gate opens, the new dispensation begins
bahai.holy_day.5.name: Declaration of the Bab
bahai.holy_day.5.theme: The Bab declares His mission as the Herald of a new age
bahai.holy_day.6.brief: Baha'u'llah's ascension — the Manifestation of God departs this world
bahai.holy_day.6.name: Ascension of Baha'u'llah
bahai.holy_day.6.theme: Passing of the Founder — solemn remembrance at 3 AM
bahai.holy_day.7.brief: The Bab's martyrdom — the Herald gave His life for the promise of a new world
bahai.holy_day.7.name: Martyrdom of the Bab
bahai.holy_day.7.theme: Execution of the Bab by firing squad in Tabriz
bahai.holy_day.8.brief: The Twin Birthdays — two consecutive days of joy for two consecutive Revelations
bahai.holy_day.8.name: Birth of the Bab / Birth of Baha'u'llah
bahai.holy_day.8.theme: Twin Holy Days celebrating the births of the Twin Manifestations
bahai.month.0.brief: The intercalary days — 4 or 5 days of generosity before the Fast
bahai.month.0.quality: Days outside the calendar devoted to hospitality, gift-giving, and charity
bahai.month.1.brief: Month of Splendour — Naw-Ruz begins the Baha'i year with light
bahai.month.1.quality: The radiance of God's revelation — the first attribute, the new year's dawn
bahai.month.10.brief: Month of Might — the strength that comes from faith, not force
bahai.month.10.quality: Spiritual power and strength — the courage of conviction
bahai.month.11.brief: Month of Will — the intersection of human choice and divine purpose
bahai.month.11.quality: Divine purpose — surrender to God's plan and active participation in it
bahai.month.12.brief: Month of Knowledge — the harmony of science and religion, twin wings of one bird
bahai.month.12.quality: The pursuit of truth through science and revelation alike
bahai.month.13.brief: Month of Power — the divine force that enables transformation
bahai.month.13.quality: Creative force — the power to transform self and society
bahai.month.14.brief: Month of Speech — words as vessels of truth or instruments of harm
bahai.month.14.quality: Truthful utterance — the responsibility and sacredness of words
bahai.month.15.brief: Month of Questions — the seeker's duty to question and discover for oneself
bahai.month.15.quality: The spirit of inquiry — independent investigation of truth
bahai.month.16.brief: Month of Honour — true nobility measured by service, not station
bahai.month.16.quality: Dignity, nobility, the honor of serving humanity
bahai.month.17.brief: Month of Sovereignty — the king of kings to whom all sovereignty belongs
bahai.month.17.quality: God's supreme authority — the recognition of a power beyond all powers
bahai.month.18.brief: Month of Dominion — the dominion that encompasses all worlds
bahai.month.18.quality: God's rule over creation — the final month before the Fast
bahai.month.19.brief: Month of Loftiness — the Fast month, 19 days of sunrise-to-sunset abstinence
bahai.month.19.quality: The highest aspiration — the month of fasting, spiritual ascent
bahai.month.2.brief: Month of Glory — the magnificence that shines through all created things
bahai.month.2.quality: The majesty and grandeur of God's creation
bahai.month.3.brief: Month of Beauty — beauty as a reflection of the divine
bahai.month.3.quality: The aesthetic perfection manifest in creation and human character
bahai.month.4.brief: Month of Grandeur — the immensity of God's creation beyond comprehension
bahai.month.4.quality: The greatness beyond measure — awe before the infinite
bahai.month.5.brief: Month of Light — the light that dispels ignorance and reveals truth
bahai.month.5.quality: Spiritual illumination, the light of knowledge and guidance
bahai.month.6.brief: Month of Mercy — the tender care of God for every creature
bahai.month.6.quality: Divine compassion and forgiveness — love in action
bahai.month.7.brief: Month of Words — the Word through which all things were created
bahai.month.7.quality: The creative Word of God — revelation as language, the power of speech
bahai.month.8.brief: Month of Perfection — the divine standard toward which humanity strives
bahai.month.8.quality: The pursuit of excellence in all things — completeness without flaw
bahai.month.9.brief: Month of Names — every divine attribute has a name, every name a world
bahai.month.9.quality: The names and attributes of God reflected in creation
buddhist.kalpa.0.description: Worlds arise from emptiness — matter condenses, beings take form
buddhist.kalpa.0.meaning: The cosmic breath inward — creation as inevitable arising
buddhist.kalpa.0.name: Formation
buddhist.kalpa.1.description: Beings live, die, and are reborn — the wheel turns
buddhist.kalpa.1.meaning: The present era — sentient experience in all its suffering and beauty
buddhist.kalpa.1.name: Existence
buddhist.kalpa.2.description: Worlds dissolve through fire, water, or wind
buddhist.kalpa.2.meaning: The cosmic breath outward — all conditioned things are impermanent
buddhist.kalpa.2.name: Destruction
buddhist.kalpa.3.description: Vast darkness between worlds — nothing remains
buddhist.kalpa.3.meaning: The great rest — potential without form, like dreamless sleep
buddhist.kalpa.3.name: Emptiness
buddhist.month.11.meaning: Month of the Sangha — community of awakening
buddhist.month.11.observance: Magha Puja — Sangha Day, 1250 monks gathered
buddhist.month.12.meaning: Month of completion — cycle prepares to renew
buddhist.month.2.meaning: The most sacred month — triple anniversary
buddhist.month.4.meaning: Month of the first teaching — the Wheel turns
buddhist.month.5.meaning: Month of withdrawal — monastics enter retreat
buddhist.month.8.meaning: Month of generosity — dana and gratitude
buddhist.month.9.meaning: Month of the path — quiet walking
buddhist.uposatha.0.meaning: An ordinary day in the cycle
buddhist.uposatha.0.name: No Observance
buddhist.uposatha.1.meaning: Darkness as potential — the seed moment before renewal
buddhist.uposatha.1.name: New Moon Observance
buddhist.uposatha.1.practice: Monastics recite Patimokkha; laity observe 8 precepts
buddhist.uposatha.2.meaning: Illumination — the Buddha's birth, enlightenment, and parinibbana all on full moons
buddhist.uposatha.2.name: Full Moon Observance
buddhist.uposatha.2.practice: Most important — full Patimokkha recital, intensive practice
buddhist.uposatha.3.meaning: Growing light — effort and aspiration building
buddhist.uposatha.3.name: Waxing Half-Moon
buddhist.uposatha.4.meaning: Releasing — wisdom of impermanence, letting go
buddhist.uposatha.4.name: Waning Half-Moon
celtic.festival.0.brief: The Celtic new year — when the veil between worlds is thinnest and the dead walk among the living
celtic.festival.0.name: Samhain
celtic.festival.0.practice: Honoring the dead, divination, releasing what has died
celtic.festival.0.reflection: What must I let die so that new life can come?
celtic.festival.0.theme: Death, ancestors, the thinning of the veil
celtic.festival.1.brief: The winter solstice — the longest night gives birth to the returning light
celtic.festival.1.name: Yule
celtic.festival.1.practice: Vigil through the longest night, lighting the Yule log, welcoming the returning sun
celtic.festival.1.reflection: What spark of hope survives in my deepest darkness?
celtic.festival.1.theme: Rebirth of light, hope in the darkest moment
celtic.festival.2.brief: Brigid's fire — the first warmth that melts the frozen ground of winter
celtic.festival.2.name: Imbolc
celtic.festival.2.practice: Brigid's cross weaving, candle lighting, blessing of seeds
celtic.festival.2.reflection: What is quickening within me that needs tending?
celtic.festival.2.theme: Purification, first stirrings of spring, dedication
celtic.festival.3.brief: The spring equinox — day and night in perfect balance, the world tips toward light
celtic.festival.3.name: Ostara
celtic.festival.3.practice: Planting seeds, spring cleaning, celebrating fertility and growth
celtic.festival.3.reflection: Where in my life do I need to restore balance?
celtic.festival.3.theme: Balance, renewal, equal light and darkness
celtic.festival.4.brief: The fire festival of union — when the earth and sky consummate the year's promise
celtic.festival.4.name: Beltane
celtic.festival.4.practice: Maypole dancing, jumping the Beltane fire, greenwood marriage
celtic.festival.4.reflection: What am I passionate about creating?
celtic.festival.4.theme: Fertility, passion, the sacred union of earth and sky
celtic.festival.5.brief: The summer solstice — the longest day, when power peaks and the Holly King begins his reign
celtic.festival.5.name: Litha
celtic.festival.5.practice: Gathering herbs at peak potency, bonfires, honoring the Oak King
celtic.festival.5.reflection: What am I celebrating, and what begins to wane?
celtic.festival.5.theme: Abundance, power at its peak, the turn toward darkness
celtic.festival.6.brief: Lugh's assembly — the first grain cut, honoring the god who sacrificed for the harvest
celtic.festival.6.name: Lughnasadh
celtic.festival.6.practice: Grain harvest, bread baking, athletic games, craft fairs
celtic.festival.6.reflection: What have I harvested this year, and what sacrifice made it possible?
celtic.festival.6.theme: First harvest, sacrifice, gratitude for abundance
celtic.festival.7.brief: The autumn equinox — day and night equal again, the descent into the dark half begins
celtic.festival.7.name: Mabon
celtic.festival.7.practice: Wine making, feast of thanksgiving, equal sharing of the harvest
celtic.festival.7.reflection: What am I grateful for as the light fades?
celtic.festival.7.theme: Second harvest, gratitude, preparation for darkness
celtic.tree.0.brief: The oldest tree — the single intercalary day when time itself holds its breath between years
celtic.tree.0.personality: Ancient wisdom, transformation, transcendence of death and rebirth
celtic.tree.1.brief: First tree to colonize bare ground — the courage to begin again in the darkest season
celtic.tree.1.personality: Initiative, new beginnings, courage to venture into the unknown
celtic.tree.10.brief: In vino veritas — the vine strips pretense and reveals the authentic self
celtic.tree.10.personality: Harvest of truth, the intoxication that reveals what is hidden
celtic.tree.10.strengths: Honesty, prophetic vision, ability to see all sides
celtic.tree.11.brief: Grows toward light through any obstacle — the spiral of persistent life
celtic.tree.11.personality: Tenacity, the ability to thrive in darkness and adversity
celtic.tree.11.strengths: Persistence, loyalty, finding light in any condition
celtic.tree.12.brief: The arrow reed — shoots straight into the depths to find what lies beneath
celtic.tree.12.challenges: Jealousy, manipulation, use of knowledge as weapon
celtic.tree.12.personality: Depth of inquiry, fearless exploration of hidden truths
celtic.tree.12.strengths: Courage, directness, ability to penetrate to the heart of a matter
celtic.tree.13.brief: The tree of the Cailleach — last month before rebirth, the elder who has seen everything
celtic.tree.13.personality: Endings, transformation, the wisdom of completion and release
celtic.tree.13.strengths: Renewal, healing, acceptance of cycles, deep compassion
celtic.tree.2.brief: The warding tree — planted at thresholds to guard against false magic and confusion
celtic.tree.2.personality: Vision, intuition, protection against enchantment and illusion
celtic.tree.2.strengths: Discernment, psychic sensitivity, courage against deception
celtic.tree.3.brief: The cosmic axis — Yggdrasil's Celtic cousin, roots in water and crown in sky
celtic.tree.3.challenges: Moody, withdrawn, lost in inner worlds
celtic.tree.3.personality: Connection between inner and outer worlds, higher perspective
celtic.tree.4.brief: The tree of fire and water — it bleeds red when cut, thrives where others drown
celtic.tree.4.personality: Determination, confidence, willingness to make difficult choices
celtic.tree.5.brief: The moon tree — bends without breaking, drinks from the deepest water
celtic.tree.5.personality: Deep emotion, intuition, the power of the feminine and lunar cycles
celtic.tree.6.brief: The fairy tree — sacred and untouchable, its beauty protected by thorns
celtic.tree.6.personality: Paradox, restraint, the coexistence of beauty and thorns
celtic.tree.6.strengths: Patience, adaptability, inner strength under a gentle exterior
celtic.tree.7.brief: The king of trees — its door (duir) opens to the light half of the year at summer solstice
celtic.tree.7.personality: Strength, endurance, generosity, protection of the vulnerable
celtic.tree.8.brief: The dark twin of Oak — rules the waning year with fierce, evergreen determination
celtic.tree.8.personality: Challenge, directness, the courage to fight for what matters
celtic.tree.9.brief: The salmon ate the hazelnuts of wisdom — the tree of concentrated knowing
celtic.tree.9.personality: Wisdom, inspiration, the flash of poetic insight
chakra.0.archetype: Grounding, safety, survival, physical vitality, belonging to the earth
chakra.0.brief: The root — LAM — the foundation upon which everything else is built
chakra.0.question: Am I safe? Do I belong here?
chakra.0.shadow: Fear, anxiety, instability, hoarding, disconnection from the body
chakra.1.archetype: Pleasure, creativity, emotional flow, sensuality, healthy desire
chakra.1.brief: The sacral — VAM — the water center where emotion and creation flow
chakra.1.question: What do I feel? What do I desire?
chakra.1.shadow: Guilt, emotional numbness, addiction, creative block, boundary issues
chakra.2.archetype: Personal power, will, confidence, self-discipline, inner fire
chakra.2.brief: The solar plexus — RAM — the fire of personal will and self-determination
chakra.2.question: Who am I? What is my purpose?
chakra.2.shadow: Shame, powerlessness, domination, anger, digestive distress
chakra.3.archetype: Unconditional love, compassion, forgiveness, connection, balance
chakra.3.brief: The heart — YAM — the unstruck sound, where human love meets the divine
chakra.3.question: Do I love? Am I loved?
chakra.3.shadow: Grief, jealousy, codependency, isolation, inability to forgive
chakra.4.archetype: Authentic expression, truth, creativity through voice, listening
chakra.4.brief: The throat — HAM — the purification center where inner truth becomes outer expression
chakra.4.question: What is my truth? Can I speak it?
chakra.4.shadow: Lies, silence, over-talking, fear of judgment, creative suppression
chakra.5.archetype: Intuition, insight, imagination, clarity, inner vision, wisdom
chakra.5.brief: The third eye — OM — the seat of intuition where duality dissolves into seeing
chakra.5.question: What do I see beyond the visible?
chakra.5.shadow: Illusion, delusion, inability to see patterns, headaches, rigidity
chakra.6.archetype: Transcendence, unity, spiritual connection, pure consciousness
chakra.6.brief: The crown — silence — the thousand-petaled lotus where self dissolves into the infinite
chakra.6.question: Am I connected to something greater?
chakra.6.shadow: Spiritual bypassing, disconnection from embodiment, nihilism, isolation
chinese.animal.0.brief: First in the cycle — the clever mind that finds opportunity in chaos
chinese.animal.0.name: الفأر
chinese.animal.1.brief: The strength that plows through — patient endurance conquers all
chinese.animal.1.name: الثور
chinese.animal.10.brief: The faithfulness that anchors — loyalty as the highest virtue
chinese.animal.10.name: الكلب
chinese.animal.11.brief: The abundance that overflows — generosity without calculation
chinese.animal.11.name: الخنزير
chinese.animal.2.brief: The roar that awakens — fearless action in service of justice
chinese.animal.2.name: النمر
chinese.animal.3.brief: The grace that disarms — softness as a form of power
chinese.animal.3.name: الأرنب
chinese.animal.4.brief: The only mythical animal — the dream of transcendence made manifest
chinese.animal.4.name: التنين
chinese.animal.5.brief: The coil that strikes true — deep wisdom beneath a still surface
chinese.animal.5.name: الأفعى
chinese.animal.6.brief: The gallop that liberates — freedom as a way of being
chinese.animal.6.name: الحصان
chinese.animal.7.brief: The heart that creates — beauty born from sensitivity
chinese.animal.7.name: الماعز
chinese.animal.8.brief: The mind that plays — genius disguised as mischief
chinese.animal.8.name: القرد
chinese.animal.9.brief: The voice that calls truth — precision in all things
chinese.animal.9.name: الديك
chinese.element.0.expression: Through creativity, vision, and upward movement
chinese.element.0.name: الخشب
chinese.element.1.name: النار
chinese.element.2.expression: Through grounding, nurture, and practical care
chinese.element.2.name: الأرض
chinese.element.3.expression: Through refinement, letting go, and sharp focus
chinese.element.3.name: المعدن
chinese.element.4.expression: Through flow, stillness, and the power of yielding
chinese.element.4.name: الماء
chinese.tpl.detail: %1$s %2$s: %3$s. القوة: %4$s. التحديات: %5$s. الأفضل مع: %6$s. العنصر: %7$s (%8$s) — %9$s. السنة %10$d من ٦٠.
convergence.pattern.0.description: Three lunar calendars mark new month together
convergence.pattern.0.name: Lunar Triple
convergence.pattern.1.name: Solar Cross
convergence.pattern.2.name: Sacred Day
convergence.pattern.3.name: New Year Cluster
convergence.pattern.4.name: East-West Bridge
convergence.pattern.5.description: Mayan portal day coincides with other markers
convergence.pattern.5.name: Dreamspell Gateway
convergence.pattern.6.description: Cosmic cycle and human calendar align
convergence.pattern.6.name: Deep Time Echo
convergence.pattern.7.name: Universal Renewal
convergence.strength.0.description: Two systems share a significant moment
convergence.strength.0.name: Minor Alignment
convergence.strength.0.significance: A whisper between traditions — coincidence or resonance
convergence.strength.1.name: Notable Convergence
convergence.strength.1.significance: Multiple traditions marking the same moment — the pattern becomes visible
convergence.strength.2.name: Major Convergence
convergence.strength.2.significance: A rare chorus — when this many traditions agree, the moment demands attention
convergence.strength.3.description: Five or more systems in alignment
convergence.strength.3.name: Rare Convergence
convergence.strength.3.significance: Exceedingly rare — the fabric of time reveals its hidden unity
convergence.system.1.what_aligns: Sign ingress or major planetary aspect
convergence.system.2.what_aligns: Kin 1 (Red Magnetic Dragon) or galactic portal day
convergence.system.4.what_aligns: New Year or first of month
convergence.system.5.what_aligns: New month (Rosh Chodesh) or High Holiday
convergence.system.6.what_aligns: New month (hilal) or sacred month boundary
coptic.feast.1.brief: The Coptic New Year — 1 Thout, colored red for the blood of the martyrs
coptic.feast.1.name: Nayrouz
coptic.feast.1.practice: Red vestments, red dates, lighting candles for the martyrs, visiting churches
coptic.feast.1.theme: New Year of the Martyrs — remembrance of those who died for faith
coptic.feast.2.brief: The Cross found — bonfires mirror the flames Helena lit to find the buried wood
coptic.feast.2.name: Feast of the Cross
coptic.feast.2.practice: Lighting bonfires (demera), singing hymns, outdoor celebration on 17 Thout
coptic.feast.2.theme: Discovery of the True Cross by Empress Helena in Jerusalem
coptic.feast.3.brief: The Word made flesh — after 43 days of fasting, the Light enters the world
coptic.feast.3.name: Christmas
coptic.feast.3.practice: Midnight liturgy (29 Koiak / January 7), breaking the fast with festive foods
coptic.feast.3.theme: The Nativity of Christ — culmination of the 43-day Advent fast
coptic.feast.4.brief: God revealed in water — the dove descends, the voice speaks, the Trinity appears
coptic.feast.4.name: Epiphany
coptic.feast.4.practice: Blessing of waters, baptismal renewal liturgy on 11 Tobi (January 19)
coptic.feast.4.theme: Theophany — the Baptism of Christ in the Jordan, God revealed
coptic.feast.5.brief: The angel's greeting — the moment when heaven asked earth's permission
coptic.feast.5.name: Annunciation
coptic.feast.5.practice: Joyful liturgy on 29 Paremhat (April 7), special hymns to the Theotokos
coptic.feast.5.theme: The angel Gabriel announces to the Virgin Mary — the Incarnation begins
coptic.feast.6.brief: The Resurrection — death defeated, the stone rolled away, the first light of Sunday
coptic.feast.6.name: Easter
coptic.feast.6.practice: 55-day Great Lent, midnight Paschal liturgy, breaking fast, red eggs, greeting 'Christ is Risen'
coptic.feast.6.theme: The Resurrection of Christ — the feast of feasts, joy of joys
coptic.month.1.brief: Month of Thoth — the ancient scribe god opens the new year of the martyrs
coptic.month.1.liturgy: Start of the Coptic year, commemorating the martyrs
coptic.month.1.name: Thout
coptic.month.1.origin: From Egyptian Thoth (Djehuty), god of wisdom and writing
coptic.month.10.brief: Month of Paoni — the ancient valley of the dead becomes the apostolic fast
coptic.month.10.liturgy: Apostles' Fast begins, honoring Peter and Paul
coptic.month.10.name: Paoni
coptic.month.10.origin: From Egyptian Pa-en-Inet (valley festival), honoring the dead
coptic.month.11.brief: Month of Epip — the fierce heat that tested both ancient Egypt and early Christians
coptic.month.11.liturgy: Summer height, commemoration of saints and martyrs
coptic.month.11.name: Epip
coptic.month.11.origin: From Egyptian Ipip, associated with the great heat
coptic.month.12.brief: Month of Mesori — the rebirth of Ra becomes the Assumption of the Theotokos
coptic.month.12.liturgy: Assumption of the Virgin Mary fast and feast
coptic.month.12.name: Mesori
coptic.month.12.origin: From Egyptian Wep-Renpet (birth of Ra), the sun reborn
coptic.month.13.brief: The little month — five or six days suspended between years, sacred pause
coptic.month.13.liturgy: Days of prayer and reflection between years, no fasting
coptic.month.13.name: Nasie
coptic.month.13.origin: Epagomenal days (Greek epagomenai), outside the regular months
coptic.month.2.brief: Month of Paopi — the sacred festival that honored the river's gift
coptic.month.2.name: Paopi
coptic.month.2.origin: From Egyptian Ipet (Opet festival), sacred barque procession
coptic.month.3.brief: Month of Hathor — the cow-goddess of joy gives way to Advent expectation
coptic.month.3.liturgy: Advent preparation begins in the Coptic tradition
coptic.month.3.name: Hathor
coptic.month.3.origin: From Egyptian Hathor, goddess of love, joy, and the sky
coptic.month.4.brief: Month of Koiak — the ancient Osiris rites become the fast before Christ's nativity
coptic.month.4.liturgy: Coptic Advent fast, preparation for Christmas (29 Koiak)
coptic.month.4.name: Koiak
coptic.month.4.origin: From Egyptian Ka-her-Ka (soul upon soul), Osiris mysteries
coptic.month.5.brief: Month of Tobi — the waters of purification and the baptism of Christ
coptic.month.5.name: Tobi
coptic.month.5.origin: From Egyptian Djebaty, associated with purification
coptic.month.6.brief: Month of Meshir — the burning sun that once parched Egypt now warms the faithful
coptic.month.6.liturgy: Ordinary time, parish festivals of local saints
coptic.month.6.name: Meshir
coptic.month.6.origin: From Egyptian Mekhir, month of the burning sun
coptic.month.7.brief: Month of Paremhat — the builder-healer's month crowned by the angel's greeting
coptic.month.7.liturgy: The Annunciation (29 Paremhat), feast of the Theotokos
coptic.month.7.name: Paremhat
coptic.month.8.brief: Month of Parmouti — the harvest goddess meets the Lenten fast
coptic.month.8.liturgy: Great Lent deepens, approaching Holy Week
coptic.month.8.name: Parmouti
coptic.month.9.brief: Month of Pashons — the moon god's month receives the light of resurrection
coptic.month.9.liturgy: Paschal season, Easter and its afterglow
coptic.month.9.name: Pashons
coptic.month.9.origin: From Egyptian Pa-en-Khonsu, moon god of passage
depth.unsupported.detail: لا يحتوي بعد على وحدة تفسير.
egyptian.epag.0.brief: Day of Osiris — the king who was dismembered and made whole, ruling the dead from his green throne
egyptian.epag.0.theme: Sacrifice, regeneration, the grain that dies to feed the world
egyptian.epag.1.brief: Day of Horus — the son who fought for his father's throne and restored Ma'at to the world
egyptian.epag.1.theme: Justice, restoration, the falcon eye that sees all
egyptian.epag.2.brief: Day of Set — the red god of the desert whose violence is the other face of creation
egyptian.epag.2.theme: Chaos, storms, the necessary opposition that defines order
egyptian.epag.3.brief: Day of Isis — the great of magic who gathered the pieces and remembered the dead back to life
egyptian.epag.3.theme: Magic, healing, the love that reassembles what was scattered
egyptian.epag.4.brief: Day of Nephthys — the mistress of the house who stands at the edge and guides souls across
egyptian.epag.4.theme: The hidden, mourning, the beauty at the boundary between worlds
egyptian.month.1.brief: First month — the ibis-headed god who invented writing and measured time itself
egyptian.month.1.name: Thoth
egyptian.month.10.brief: Month of the divine mother — she who consumes death and births eternity
egyptian.month.10.domain: The great mother, queenship, the vulture
egyptian.month.10.name: Payni
egyptian.month.11.brief: Month of the cobra crown — the fierce eye of Ra that guards the throne
egyptian.month.11.name: Epiphi
egyptian.month.12.brief: Month of the birth of Ra — the sun reborn at the peak of its power
egyptian.month.12.domain: The sun at zenith, creation, sovereignty
egyptian.month.12.name: Mesore
egyptian.month.2.brief: Month of the golden goddess — joy and celebration as the flood settles
egyptian.month.2.name: Phaophi
egyptian.month.3.brief: Month of the lioness — the fierce protector who can both destroy and heal
egyptian.month.3.name: Athyr
egyptian.month.4.brief: Month of the slain king — the mystery of death that feeds all life
egyptian.month.4.name: Choiak
egyptian.month.5.brief: Month of emergence — the potent seed that breaks through the dark earth
egyptian.month.5.name: Tybi
egyptian.month.6.brief: Month of great burning — the fire that purifies and the light that reveals
egyptian.month.6.name: Mechir
egyptian.month.7.brief: Month of the builder — the deified architect who measured heaven and earth
egyptian.month.7.name: Phamenoth
egyptian.month.8.brief: Month of the harvest goddess — the serpent who guards the granary
egyptian.month.8.name: Pharmuthi
egyptian.month.9.brief: Month of the moon child — the wanderer who traverses the sky
egyptian.month.9.domain: The moon, healing, passage of time
egyptian.month.9.name: Pachons
egyptian.season.0.brief: The great flooding — destruction and renewal united in the same dark water
egyptian.season.0.name: Akhet
egyptian.season.0.reflection: Surrender to forces beyond control — what must be submerged so new life can grow?
egyptian.season.0.theme: The Nile floods, covering the land with fertile black silt
egyptian.season.1.brief: The coming forth — life pushes through the wet earth after the flood
egyptian.season.1.name: Peret
egyptian.season.1.reflection: The ground has been prepared — what seeds are you planting in the fertile aftermath?
egyptian.season.1.theme: The waters recede, seeds are planted in the rich new soil
egyptian.season.2.brief: The low water — the sun burns away everything but what has taken root
egyptian.season.2.name: Shemu
egyptian.season.2.reflection: What has ripened in you that is ready to be gathered and shared?
egyptian.season.2.theme: The crops are gathered, the land dries under the blazing sun
egyptian.season.3.brief: The five days upon the year — when time pauses and the divine breaks through
egyptian.season.3.name: Epagomenal
egyptian.season.3.reflection: Between endings and beginnings — the liminal space where gods are born
egyptian.season.3.theme: Five days between years, sacred and dangerous, belonging to no month
ethiopian.feast.1.brief: Ethiopian New Year — 1 Meskerem, when the land blooms gold and children sing for blessings
ethiopian.feast.1.name: Enkutatash
ethiopian.feast.1.practice: Yellow daisies (adey abeba), bonfires (chibo), singing door to door, new clothes, feasting
ethiopian.feast.1.theme: New Year — gift of jewels, the return of the Queen of Sheba
ethiopian.feast.2.brief: The Cross revealed — 17 Meskerem, when the great bonfire falls toward the buried relic
ethiopian.feast.2.name: Meskel
ethiopian.feast.2.practice: Demera bonfire ceremony, yellow daisies laid in cross pattern, processions with priests and drums
ethiopian.feast.2.theme: Finding of the True Cross — light triumphant over darkness
ethiopian.feast.3.brief: Ethiopian Christmas — 29 Tahsas, the dawn service after the longest fast
ethiopian.feast.3.name: Genna
ethiopian.feast.3.practice: All-night church service, breaking the fast, genna (field hockey), white shammas, injera feast
ethiopian.feast.3.theme: Christmas — the Nativity, preceded by 43 days of fasting
ethiopian.feast.4.brief: Timkat — 11 Tir, when the tabots emerge from the churches and the waters are blessed
ethiopian.feast.4.name: Timkat
ethiopian.feast.4.practice: Three-day festival, tabot (Ark replica) processions, blessing of waters, joyful crowds in white
ethiopian.feast.4.theme: Epiphany — the Baptism of Christ, the holiest celebration
ethiopian.month.1.brief: First month — the rains recede and Ethiopia carpets itself in yellow daisies
ethiopian.month.1.name: Meskerem
ethiopian.month.1.season: End of rainy season (Kiremt), flowers bloom across the highlands
ethiopian.month.10.brief: Tenth month — the great rains arrive, rivers swell, the world goes green
ethiopian.month.10.name: Sene
ethiopian.month.10.season: Kiremt (main rainy season) begins, heavy monsoon
ethiopian.month.11.brief: Eleventh month — the rains at their most fierce, the highlands draped in mist
ethiopian.month.11.name: Hamle
ethiopian.month.11.season: Heaviest rains, some flooding, lush green highlands
ethiopian.month.12.brief: Twelfth month — the rains slow their drumming, Meskerem approaches
ethiopian.month.12.name: Nehase
ethiopian.month.12.season: Rains begin to taper, preparing for the new year
ethiopian.month.13.brief: The little month — the days outside time, when the old year surrenders to the new
ethiopian.month.13.name: Pagume
ethiopian.month.13.season: Five or six extra days between years, sacred transition
ethiopian.month.2.brief: Second month — the fertile earth receives its seeds after the great rains
ethiopian.month.2.name: Tikimt
ethiopian.month.2.season: Post-rain planting season, the land is rich and ready
ethiopian.month.3.brief: Third month — the gathering in, as autumn deepens on the highland plateau
ethiopian.month.3.name: Hidar
ethiopian.month.3.season: Short rains possible, days shorten, harvest preparation
ethiopian.month.4.brief: Fourth month — the month of joy that builds toward Genna
ethiopian.month.4.name: Tahsas
ethiopian.month.5.brief: Fifth month — the sun blazes and Timkat processions fill the streets with color
ethiopian.month.5.name: Tir
ethiopian.month.6.brief: Sixth month — what was planted begins to stir beneath the dry earth
ethiopian.month.6.name: Yekatit
ethiopian.month.6.season: Dry season, seeds germinating from earlier planting
ethiopian.month.7.brief: Seventh month — the small rains arrive, the first green returns
ethiopian.month.7.name: Megabit
ethiopian.month.7.season: Small rains (Belg) may begin in some regions
ethiopian.month.8.brief: Eighth month — the blessing of rain and the joy of Fasika (Easter)
ethiopian.month.8.name: Miyazia
ethiopian.month.8.season: Belg rains, Easter season, the earth greens
ethiopian.month.9.brief: Ninth month — everything grows with force as the big rains draw near
ethiopian.month.9.name: Ginbot
ethiopian.month.9.season: Main rains approaching, strong vegetative growth
french_republican.month.1.brief: Month of the grape harvest — the Revolution began at vintage time
french_republican.month.1.nature: Grapes heavy on the vine, wine-making, the harvest of intoxication
french_republican.month.10.brief: Month of harvest — the golden reward of patience and labor
french_republican.month.10.nature: Grain harvest, golden fields, the reward of the year's labor
french_republican.month.11.brief: Month of heat — 9 Thermidor ended Robespierre and the Reign of Terror
french_republican.month.11.nature: Peak heat, parching sun, the coup that ended the Terror
french_republican.month.12.brief: Month of fruit — the year's sweetest gifts before the cycle turns again
french_republican.month.12.nature: Fruit ripening, orchards heavy, the sweet conclusion of summer
french_republican.month.2.brief: Month of fog — Napoleon seized power on 18 Brumaire
french_republican.month.2.nature: Morning mists, the world hidden and revealed, the breath of cooling earth
french_republican.month.3.brief: Month of frost — the cold that ends the growing season and begins the waiting
french_republican.month.3.nature: First frost, fields whitening at dawn, the earth hardening
french_republican.month.4.brief: Month of snow — the world gone white and still
french_republican.month.4.nature: Snow covering the land, silence, the white blanket of rest
french_republican.month.5.brief: Month of rain — the waters that dissolve winter and feed spring
french_republican.month.5.nature: Winter rains, the persistence of water, melting at the margins
french_republican.month.6.brief: Month of wind — the breath that sweeps away winter and clears the air
french_republican.month.6.nature: March winds, the last storms before spring, the world stirring
french_republican.month.7.brief: Month of germination — the name Zola chose for revolution rising from the earth
french_republican.month.7.nature: Seeds sprouting, first green shoots, the earth opening
french_republican.month.8.brief: Month of flowers — nature's most lavish display of color and scent
french_republican.month.8.nature: Flowers everywhere, orchards in blossom, the extravagance of spring
french_republican.month.9.brief: Month of meadows — the fields at their greenest, ready for the scythe
french_republican.month.9.nature: Meadow grasses tall, haymaking, the green abundance
french_republican.sans.0.brief: Day of Virtue — moral excellence, the foundation of the Republic
french_republican.sans.1.brief: Day of Genius — creative brilliance, reason's triumph over superstition
french_republican.sans.2.brief: Day of Labor — the dignity of work, the hands that build the nation
french_republican.sans.3.brief: Day of Opinion — free speech, the right to think and speak without fear
french_republican.sans.4.brief: Day of Rewards — honoring those who served the Republic with distinction
french_republican.sans.5.brief: Day of Revolution — the leap year day, celebrating the act that changed the world
geology.eon.0.brief: The age of fire — 4.54 to 4.0 billion years ago, Earth forming from the solar nebula
geology.eon.0.character: Molten hell — the bombardment era when Earth was a ball of magma under constant asteroid rain
geology.eon.0.life: No life — surface too hot for water, atmosphere of hydrogen and helium
geology.eon.0.name: Hadean
geology.eon.1.brief: The age of beginnings — 4.0 to 2.5 Ga, when life invented itself in a world without oxygen
geology.eon.1.character: First oceans, first continents — the planet cools enough for liquid water and the first rocks
geology.eon.1.life: First life appears — prokaryotes, stromatolites, the Great Oxidation Event begins
geology.eon.1.name: Archean
geology.eon.2.brief: The age of hidden life — 2.5 Ga to 538 Ma, the longest eon, oxygen slowly transforming everything
geology.eon.2.character: Snowball Earth episodes, oxygen rising — the long preparation for complex life
geology.eon.2.life: Eukaryotes evolve, first multicellular organisms — the Ediacaran fauna at the end
geology.eon.2.name: Proterozoic
geology.eon.3.brief: The age of visible life — 538 Ma to present, everything we recognize as life
geology.eon.3.character: The age we live in — all familiar life, all mass extinctions, all human history
geology.eon.3.life: Cambrian explosion to now — trilobites, dinosaurs, mammals, humans, all in 538 million years
geology.eon.3.name: Phanerozoic
geology.extinction.0.aftermath: Recovery took 5-10 million years; life diversified anew in the Silurian
geology.extinction.0.brief: The ice extinction — 85% of marine species lost when Gondwana froze at the South Pole
geology.extinction.0.cause: Glaciation of Gondwana, sea level drop, ocean anoxia
geology.extinction.0.name: Late Ordovician
geology.extinction.1.aftermath: Armored fish disappeared; tetrapods (our ancestors) survived and walked onto land
geology.extinction.1.brief: The slow extinction — 75% of species lost in pulses over 20 million years
geology.extinction.1.cause: Multiple pulses over 20 My: ocean anoxia, possible volcanism, Appalachian weathering
geology.extinction.1.name: Late Devonian
geology.extinction.2.aftermath: Recovery took 10 million years; the emptied world was inherited by archosaurs leading to dinosaurs
geology.extinction.2.brief: The Great Dying — 96% of all species lost, the worst catastrophe life has ever faced
geology.extinction.2.cause: Siberian Traps volcanism, global warming, ocean acidification and anoxia
geology.extinction.2.name: End-Permian
geology.extinction.3.aftermath: Dinosaurs inherited the emptied niches, beginning their 135-million-year reign
geology.extinction.3.brief: The dinosaur's opportunity — 80% of species lost, clearing the stage for the Jurassic giants
geology.extinction.3.cause: Central Atlantic Magmatic Province volcanism, CO2 spike, ocean acidification
geology.extinction.3.name: End-Triassic
geology.extinction.4.aftermath: Mammals diversified explosively; birds survived as the last dinosaurs; our lineage began
geology.extinction.4.brief: The asteroid — 76% of species including all non-avian dinosaurs, ending one world and beginning ours
geology.extinction.4.cause: Chicxulub asteroid impact + Deccan Traps volcanism, impact winter, global fires
geology.extinction.4.name: End-Cretaceous
geology.supercontinent.0.brief: The first gathering — Earth's earliest attempt to assemble its crust into one mass
geology.supercontinent.0.description: Oldest proposed supercontinent — evidence from South Africa (Kaapvaal) and Australia (Pilbara)
geology.supercontinent.0.name: Vaalbara
geology.supercontinent.1.brief: The continent that breathed — its breakup may have triggered the rise of atmospheric oxygen
geology.supercontinent.1.description: Archean supercontinent that preceded the Great Oxidation Event
geology.supercontinent.1.name: Kenorland
geology.supercontinent.2.brief: The first proven unity — Earth's continents locked together for 500 million years
geology.supercontinent.2.description: Also called Nuna — first well-documented supercontinent, all major cratons joined
geology.supercontinent.2.name: Columbia
geology.supercontinent.3.brief: The continent that froze the world — its rifting led to the most extreme ice ages
geology.supercontinent.3.description: Supercontinent whose breakup may have triggered Snowball Earth glaciations
geology.supercontinent.3.name: Rodinia
geology.supercontinent.4.brief: The fleeting assembly — together and apart in 80 million years, bookending the first complex animals
geology.supercontinent.4.description: Brief assembly at the end of the Ediacaran, just before the Cambrian explosion
geology.supercontinent.4.name: Pannotia
geology.supercontinent.5.brief: The one we remember — the supercontinent that shaped the world we know
geology.supercontinent.5.description: The most famous supercontinent — Permian to Jurassic, all land united
geology.supercontinent.5.name: Pangaea
geology.supercontinent.6.brief: The one to come — the Atlantic will close and the continents will embrace again
geology.supercontinent.6.description: Predicted next supercontinent — Atlantic closing, new assembly in 250 million years
geology.supercontinent.6.name: Pangaea Proxima
gregorian.day.0.brief: The Moon's day — intuition, emotion, the tides within
gregorian.day.0.name: الاثنين
gregorian.day.1.brief: Mars' day — courage, action, the warrior's resolve
gregorian.day.1.name: الثلاثاء
gregorian.day.1.origin: Old English Tiwesdaeg (Tyr's day, Norse Mars)
gregorian.day.2.brief: Mercury's day — communication, craft, the trickster's wit
gregorian.day.2.name: الأربعاء
gregorian.day.2.origin: Old English Wodnesdaeg (Woden's day, Norse Mercury)
gregorian.day.3.brief: Jupiter's day — expansion, abundance, the thunder god's generosity
gregorian.day.3.name: الخميس
gregorian.day.3.origin: Old English Thunresdaeg (Thor's day, Norse Jupiter)
gregorian.day.4.brief: Venus' day — love, beauty, the goddess of desire
gregorian.day.4.name: الجمعة
gregorian.day.4.origin: Old English Frigedaeg (Freya's day, Norse Venus)
gregorian.day.5.brief: Saturn's day — discipline, structure, the taskmaster's lesson
gregorian.day.5.name: السبت
gregorian.day.6.brief: The Sun's day — vitality, sovereignty, the center of all orbits
gregorian.day.6.name: الأحد
gregorian.month.1.brief: The month of doorways — one face looks back, one looks forward
gregorian.month.1.name: يناير
gregorian.month.1.origin: Janus — two-faced god of beginnings, gates, and transitions
gregorian.month.10.brief: The eighth month — the world turns inward as the light contracts
gregorian.month.10.name: أكتوبر
gregorian.month.10.origin: Octo (eight) — the eighth month of the old Roman calendar
gregorian.month.11.brief: The ninth month — the dead are honored as the year grows old
gregorian.month.11.name: نوفمبر
gregorian.month.11.origin: Novem (nine) — the ninth month of the old Roman calendar
gregorian.month.11.quality: Late autumn, remembrance, preparation for dark
gregorian.month.12.brief: The tenth month — in the deepest dark, the ancient Romans feasted and the sun was reborn
gregorian.month.12.name: ديسمبر
gregorian.month.12.origin: Decem (ten) — the tenth month of the old Roman calendar
gregorian.month.12.quality: Winter solstice, Saturnalia, the return of light
gregorian.month.2.brief: The month of cleansing — the last fires of purification before new growth
gregorian.month.2.name: فبراير
gregorian.month.2.quality: Late winter, purification, preparation for spring
gregorian.month.3.brief: The month of Mars — the old Roman year began here, with war and planting alike
gregorian.month.3.name: مارس
gregorian.month.3.origin: Mars — god of war and agriculture, the Roman new year
gregorian.month.3.quality: Spring equinox, awakening, aggressive new growth
gregorian.month.4.brief: The month of opening — everything that was closed begins to unfold
gregorian.month.4.name: أبريل
gregorian.month.4.origin: Aperire (to open) — the earth opens, buds unfold
gregorian.month.5.brief: The month of growth — the earth goddess whose name means 'she who is great'
gregorian.month.5.name: مايو
gregorian.month.5.origin: Maia — goddess of growth, mother of Mercury
gregorian.month.6.brief: The month of marriage — the queen of heaven blesses the longest days
gregorian.month.6.name: يونيو
gregorian.month.6.origin: Juno — queen of the gods, patron of marriage
gregorian.month.7.brief: The month of Caesar — named for the man who remade time itself
gregorian.month.7.name: يوليو
gregorian.month.7.origin: Julius Caesar — reformed the calendar, deified after death
gregorian.month.8.brief: The month of Augustus — the emperor who demanded a month as grand as July
gregorian.month.8.name: أغسطس
gregorian.month.8.origin: Augustus Caesar — first Roman emperor, 'the revered one'
gregorian.month.8.quality: Late summer, harvest begins, empire at zenith
gregorian.month.9.brief: The seventh month — still carrying its old number though it is now the ninth
gregorian.month.9.name: سبتمبر
gregorian.month.9.origin: Septem (seven) — the seventh month of the old Roman calendar
gregorian.season.0.brief: The season of becoming — everything that slept now wakes
gregorian.season.0.name: الربيع
gregorian.season.0.quality: Expansive — energy rises, seeds germinate, days lengthen
gregorian.season.1.brief: The season of being — the world at maximum brilliance
gregorian.season.1.name: الصيف
gregorian.season.1.quality: Radiant — energy at peak, light dominates, life in full bloom
gregorian.season.2.brief: The season of letting go — beauty in decay, richness in release
gregorian.season.2.name: الخريف
gregorian.season.2.quality: Contracting — energy turns inward, fruit ripens, leaves fall
gregorian.season.2.theme: Harvest, release, gratitude for what grew
gregorian.season.3.brief: The season of rest — what appears dead is dreaming
gregorian.season.3.name: الشتاء
gregorian.season.3.quality: Still — energy withdrawn to roots, silence, gestation in darkness
gregorian.season.3.theme: Rest, depth, the hidden seed, essential stillness
hd.auth.0.brief: Decisions emerge from emotional patience — there is no truth in the now
hd.auth.0.name: Emotional
hd.auth.0.process: Ride the emotional wave — clarity comes over time, never in the moment
hd.auth.0.signal: Wait for emotional clarity across highs and lows before deciding
hd.auth.1.brief: The body knows before the mind — respond to life through sacral sounds
hd.auth.1.name: Sacral
hd.auth.1.process: Listen to the gut response — an immediate uh-huh or uhn-uhn
hd.auth.1.signal: A visceral, in-the-body yes or no that bypasses the mind
hd.auth.2.brief: Survival intelligence speaks in the moment — trust the first instinct
hd.auth.2.name: Splenic
hd.auth.2.process: Trust the spontaneous knowing — the spleen speaks once and softly
hd.auth.2.signal: A quiet, instantaneous hit of intuition that does not repeat
hd.auth.3.brief: The heart center drives commitment — only promise what the ego can sustain
hd.auth.3.name: Ego
hd.auth.3.process: Honor what the heart truly wants — willpower aligned with desire
hd.auth.3.signal: A sense of what you have the will and heart to commit to
hd.auth.4.brief: Identity speaks through the voice — talk it through with trusted others
hd.auth.4.name: Self-Projected
hd.auth.4.process: Hear yourself speak your truth — the voice reveals direction
hd.auth.4.signal: Listen to what comes out of your mouth when you speak about the decision
hd.auth.5.brief: No defined inner authority — decisions emerge through dialogue and environment
hd.auth.5.name: Mental
hd.auth.5.process: Use the mind as a sounding board — no inner authority, rely on environment
hd.auth.5.signal: Discuss with others and notice what feels right in different environments
hd.auth.6.brief: The moon's transit through all 64 gates reveals clarity — patience is everything
hd.auth.6.name: Lunar
hd.auth.6.process: Wait through a full 28-day lunar cycle — sample all transits before deciding
hd.auth.6.signal: After a full moon cycle, notice which option consistently felt right
hd.center.0.defined: Consistent mental pressure to think, question, and wonder
hd.center.0.name: Head
hd.center.0.open: Amplifies others' inspiration — overwhelmed by questions that aren't yours
hd.center.0.question: Am I trying to answer everybody else's questions?
hd.center.1.defined: Fixed way of processing and conceptualizing information
hd.center.1.name: Ajna
hd.center.1.open: Open to all ways of thinking — wisdom is mental flexibility
hd.center.1.question: Am I trying to convince everyone I'm certain?
hd.center.2.defined: Consistent voice and capacity to manifest through expression
hd.center.2.name: Throat
hd.center.2.open: Adapts communication to the audience — pressure to speak or act prematurely
hd.center.2.question: Am I trying to attract attention?
hd.center.3.defined: Fixed sense of identity, direction, and love
hd.center.3.name: G Center
hd.center.3.open: Shape-shifts identity depending on environment and companions
hd.center.3.question: Am I still looking for love and direction?
hd.center.4.defined: Reliable willpower and capacity for material promises
hd.center.4.name: Heart
hd.center.4.open: Inconsistent willpower — wisdom is not needing to prove self-worth
hd.center.4.question: Am I still trying to prove myself?
hd.center.5.defined: Consistent intuition, immune response, and survival awareness
hd.center.5.name: Spleen
hd.center.5.open: Amplifies health and fear signals — holds on to what should be released
hd.center.5.question: Am I still holding on to what isn't good for me?
hd.center.6.defined: Sustainable life force and work energy — the Generator's engine
hd.center.6.name: Sacral
hd.center.6.open: Inconsistent energy — wisdom is knowing when enough is enough
hd.center.6.question: Do I know when enough is enough?
hd.center.7.defined: Emotional wave that brings depth, passion, and feeling to experience
hd.center.7.name: Solar Plexus
hd.center.7.open: Empathic amplifier — absorbs and magnifies the emotions of others
hd.center.7.question: Am I avoiding confrontation and truth?
hd.center.8.defined: Consistent adrenal pressure that drives action and momentum
hd.center.8.name: Root
hd.center.8.open: Amplifies stress — pressure to hurry and be free of the pressure
hd.center.8.question: Am I in a hurry to be free of the pressure?
hd.profile.0.brief: The deeply experiential researcher — builds certainty through investigation and breakdowns
hd.profile.0.conscious: Investigator — driven to build a secure foundation of knowledge
hd.profile.0.unconscious: Martyr — learns through trial and error, discovering what doesn't work
hd.profile.1.brief: The networked scholar — deep research shared through close community
hd.profile.1.conscious: Investigator — needs a solid knowledge foundation before acting
hd.profile.1.unconscious: Opportunist — opportunities flow through personal networks and relationships
hd.profile.10.brief: The wise recluse — three-phase maturation with natural gifts that emerge alone
hd.profile.10.conscious: Role Model — three life phases: trial, observation, embodiment
hd.profile.10.unconscious: Hermit — unconscious natural gift that flourishes in solitude
hd.profile.11.brief: The experienced optimist — double trial process that ultimately models authentic living
hd.profile.11.conscious: Role Model — three-phase life journey toward authentic embodiment
hd.profile.11.unconscious: Martyr — unconscious drive to discover through trial and error
hd.profile.2.brief: The naturally gifted friend — talent emerges when called by the right people
hd.profile.2.conscious: Hermit — possesses natural talent that needs calling out by others
hd.profile.2.unconscious: Opportunist — life moves through relationship networks
hd.profile.3.brief: The reluctant savior — called from isolation to deliver practical solutions
hd.profile.3.conscious: Hermit — natural gifts that require solitude to develop
hd.profile.3.unconscious: Heretic — universalizing projections attract practical solutions from others
hd.profile.4.brief: The resilient problem-solver — experience-born wisdom that others seek out
hd.profile.4.conscious: Martyr — learns through bonds made and broken, trial and error
hd.profile.4.unconscious: Heretic — projected upon as having universal answers
hd.profile.5.brief: The experiential sage — hard-won wisdom that eventually becomes living example
hd.profile.5.conscious: Martyr — discovers through experimentation and broken bonds
hd.profile.5.unconscious: Role Model — three life phases: trial (to 30), observer (30-50), exemplar (50+)
hd.profile.6.brief: The trusted mentor — community-rooted guidance that matures into role model leadership
hd.profile.6.conscious: Opportunist — influence flows through close community bonds
hd.profile.6.unconscious: Role Model — three life phases: trial, retreat to the roof, then embodied wisdom
hd.profile.7.brief: The fixed foundation networker — once the foundation is set, it does not change
hd.profile.7.conscious: Opportunist — life direction comes through relationships
hd.profile.7.unconscious: Investigator — unconscious need for deep, authoritative foundation
hd.profile.8.brief: The practical general — deeply researched solutions delivered to meet projections
hd.profile.8.conscious: Heretic — others project universal solutions onto you
hd.profile.8.unconscious: Investigator — unconscious need to deeply research before delivering
hd.profile.9.brief: The called savior — natural talent projected upon by others, needing retreat
hd.profile.9.conscious: Heretic — projected upon as having the answer
hd.profile.9.unconscious: Hermit — unconscious natural talent that needs withdrawal to recharge

hd.type.0.aura: Closed and repelling — pushes energy outward to initiate
hd.type.0.brief: The initiator who sets things in motion — designed to act independently and inform others
hd.type.0.name: Manifestor
hd.type.1.aura: Open and enveloping — draws life toward it magnetically
hd.type.1.brief: The life force of the planet — designed to find the right work through gut response
hd.type.1.name: Generator
hd.type.2.aura: Open and enveloping — draws life while accelerating toward action
hd.type.2.brief: The multi-passionate builder — responds first, then initiates with sustainable energy
hd.type.2.name: Manifesting Generator
hd.type.3.aura: Focused and absorbing — penetrates into the other's aura to see deeply
hd.type.3.brief: The guide who sees others clearly — designed to direct energy, not generate it
hd.type.3.name: Projector
hd.type.4.aura: Resistant and sampling — reflects the health of the community
hd.type.4.brief: The mirror of the community — designed to evaluate by reflecting the world through a full lunar cycle
hd.type.4.name: Reflector

iching.hex.1.image: Heaven upon heaven — the sage strengthens himself ceaselessly
iching.hex.1.judgment: Pure creative force initiates all things
iching.hex.1.name: The Creative
iching.hex.10.image: Heaven above the lake — the sage distinguishes high and low
iching.hex.10.judgment: Treading upon the tiger's tail with care and propriety brings success
iching.hex.10.name: Treading
iching.hex.11.image: Earth above heaven — the ruler channels the way of heaven for the people
iching.hex.11.judgment: Heaven and earth unite — the small departs, the great approaches
iching.hex.11.name: Peace
iching.hex.12.image: Heaven above earth — the sage withdraws into inner worth
iching.hex.12.judgment: Heaven and earth do not communicate — decline and obstruction
iching.hex.12.name: Standstill
iching.hex.13.image: Heaven with fire — the sage organizes clans and distinguishes things
iching.hex.13.judgment: True fellowship among people through shared principles
iching.hex.13.name: Fellowship
iching.hex.14.image: Fire above heaven — the sage curbs evil and promotes good
iching.hex.14.judgment: Fire in heaven — supreme success through understanding what is great
iching.hex.14.name: Great Possession
iching.hex.15.image: Mountain within earth — the sage reduces what is too much and increases what is too little
iching.hex.15.judgment: Modesty creates success — the mountain within the earth
iching.hex.15.name: Modesty
iching.hex.16.image: Thunder over earth — the ancient kings made music to honor merit
iching.hex.16.judgment: Thunder over earth — enthusiasm moves all things
iching.hex.16.name: Enthusiasm
iching.hex.17.image: Thunder within the lake — the sage rests at nightfall
iching.hex.17.judgment: Adapting to the time and following with sincerity brings success
iching.hex.17.name: Following
iching.hex.18.image: Wind at the foot of the mountain — the sage stirs up the people and nourishes virtue
iching.hex.18.judgment: What has been corrupted through neglect can be restored through effort
iching.hex.18.name: Work on the Decayed
iching.hex.19.image: Earth above the lake — the sage teaches and shelters without limit
iching.hex.19.judgment: The great approaches with willing condescension toward the small
iching.hex.19.name: Approach
iching.hex.2.image: Earth upon earth — the sage supports all beings with broad virtue
iching.hex.2.judgment: Pure receptive force nourishes all things
iching.hex.2.name: The Receptive
iching.hex.20.image: Wind over earth — the ancient kings visited the regions to teach the people
iching.hex.20.judgment: The sage gazes upon the divine meaning beneath appearances
iching.hex.20.name: Contemplation
iching.hex.21.image: Thunder and lightning — the ancient kings enforced the laws with clarity
iching.hex.21.judgment: Energetic biting through obstacles restores unity
iching.hex.21.name: Biting Through
iching.hex.22.image: Fire at the foot of the mountain — the sage brightens governance without daring to judge
iching.hex.22.judgment: Beauty adorns form but substance must remain within
iching.hex.22.name: Grace
iching.hex.23.image: Mountain resting on the earth — the ruler strengthens the foundation of the people
iching.hex.23.judgment: The dark forces peel away what has been built — accept and wait
iching.hex.23.name: Splitting Apart
iching.hex.24.image: Thunder within the earth — the ancient kings closed the passes at solstice
iching.hex.24.judgment: After decay, the turning point — the one light returns below
iching.hex.24.name: Return
iching.hex.25.image: Thunder under heaven — the ancient kings nourished all beings in accord with the season
iching.hex.25.judgment: Acting from genuine nature without ulterior motive brings success
iching.hex.25.name: Innocence
iching.hex.26.image: Heaven within the mountain — the sage studies the ancients to cultivate character
iching.hex.26.judgment: Firm restraint of great creative power builds reserves of strength
iching.hex.26.name: Great Taming
iching.hex.27.image: Thunder at the foot of the mountain — the sage is careful with words and temperate in eating
iching.hex.27.judgment: Attending to what nourishes body and mind brings good fortune
iching.hex.27.name: Nourishment
iching.hex.28.image: The lake rises above the trees — the sage stands alone without fear
iching.hex.28.judgment: The ridgepole bends — extraordinary measures are needed in extraordinary times
iching.hex.28.name: Great Excess
iching.hex.29.image: Water flows on and reaches the goal — the sage teaches through repeated practice
iching.hex.29.judgment: Repeated danger teaches sincerity of heart — flow like water
iching.hex.29.name: The Abysmal
iching.hex.3.image: Thunder and rain — the sage brings order from chaos
iching.hex.3.judgment: Birth pangs of new endeavors require perseverance
iching.hex.3.name: Difficulty at the Beginning
iching.hex.30.image: Fire doubled produces great brightness — the sage illuminates all regions
iching.hex.30.judgment: Luminous clarity depends on something to cling to — accept dependence
iching.hex.30.name: The Clinging
iching.hex.31.image: The lake upon the mountain — the sage receives others with openness
iching.hex.31.judgment: Mutual attraction between the yielding and the firm brings union
iching.hex.31.name: Influence
iching.hex.32.image: Thunder and wind reinforce each other — the sage stands firm in his direction
iching.hex.32.judgment: Enduring consistency in one's way of life brings success
iching.hex.32.name: Duration
iching.hex.33.image: Mountain under heaven — the sage keeps the inferior at a distance with dignity
iching.hex.33.judgment: Strategic withdrawal before the advancing dark preserves strength
iching.hex.33.name: Retreat
iching.hex.34.image: Thunder above heaven — the sage treads no path contrary to propriety
iching.hex.34.judgment: Great power combined with righteousness moves heaven and earth
iching.hex.34.name: Great Power
iching.hex.35.image: The sun rises over the earth — the sage brightens his bright virtue
iching.hex.35.judgment: The bright sun rises over the earth — willing service brings rapid advance
iching.hex.35.name: Progress
iching.hex.36.image: The sun sinks into the earth — the sage veils his brightness in dealings with the multitude
iching.hex.36.judgment: In times of darkness the sage conceals his light to endure adversity
iching.hex.36.name: Darkening of the Light
iching.hex.37.image: Wind comes from fire — the sage speaks with substance and acts with consistency
iching.hex.37.judgment: The well-ordered family is the foundation of all social order
iching.hex.37.name: The Family
iching.hex.38.image: Fire above the lake — the sage remains individual within community
iching.hex.38.judgment: Despite outward opposition, underlying affinity makes small things possible
iching.hex.38.name: Opposition
iching.hex.39.image: Water on the mountain — the sage turns attention to self-improvement
iching.hex.39.judgment: Facing an abyss before and a mountain behind — turn inward and gather allies
iching.hex.39.name: Obstruction
iching.hex.4.image: Spring at the foot of the mountain — the sage cultivates character
iching.hex.4.judgment: The young fool seeks the teacher, not the teacher the fool
iching.hex.4.name: Youthful Folly
iching.hex.40.image: Thunder and rain arise — the sage pardons errors and forgives transgressions
iching.hex.40.judgment: When release from tension comes, return to normalcy swiftly
iching.hex.40.name: Deliverance
iching.hex.41.image: The lake at the foot of the mountain — the sage controls anger and restrains instincts
iching.hex.41.judgment: Decrease of the lower enriches the higher — sincerity in simplicity brings blessings
iching.hex.41.name: Decrease
iching.hex.42.image: Wind and thunder strengthen each other — the sage moves toward good and corrects faults
iching.hex.42.judgment: The ruler decreases self to increase the people — generous sacrifice brings benefit
iching.hex.42.name: Increase
iching.hex.43.image: The lake rises to heaven — the sage distributes blessings to those below
iching.hex.43.judgment: Resolute action brings dark matters to light — proclaim the danger openly
iching.hex.43.name: Breakthrough
iching.hex.44.image: Wind under heaven — the ruler proclaims his commands to the four quarters
iching.hex.44.judgment: The dark principle advances from below — recognize and contain it early
iching.hex.44.name: Coming to Meet
iching.hex.45.image: The lake over the earth — the sage renews his weapons to meet the unforeseen
iching.hex.45.judgment: People gather around a strong leader — prepare for the unexpected
iching.hex.45.name: Gathering Together
iching.hex.46.image: Within the earth wood grows — the sage builds great things from small beginnings
iching.hex.46.judgment: Gentle effort directed steadily upward meets with supreme success
iching.hex.46.name: Pushing Upward
iching.hex.47.image: The lake has no water — the sage risks his life to fulfill his purpose
iching.hex.47.judgment: Exhaustion of resources tests character — the sage stakes his life on following his will
iching.hex.47.name: Oppression
iching.hex.48.image: Water over wood — the sage encourages the people and counsels mutual aid
iching.hex.48.judgment: The well nourishes all without being exhausted — seek the unchanging source
iching.hex.48.name: The Well
iching.hex.49.image: Fire in the lake — the sage orders the calendar and clarifies the seasons
iching.hex.49.judgment: When the time is ripe, revolutionary change commands belief
iching.hex.49.name: Revolution
iching.hex.5.image: Clouds rise to heaven — the sage eats, drinks, and is joyful
iching.hex.5.judgment: Sincerity and patience lead to crossing the great water
iching.hex.5.name: Waiting
iching.hex.50.image: Fire over wood — the sage consolidates destiny by making his position correct
iching.hex.50.judgment: The sacred vessel transforms raw material into nourishment for the wise
iching.hex.50.name: The Cauldron
iching.hex.51.image: Repeated thunder — the sage sets things in order through fearful reflection
iching.hex.51.judgment: Shock and thunder bring fear, then laughter — the sage examines himself through awe
iching.hex.51.name: The Arousing
iching.hex.52.image: Mountains joined together — the sage does not permit thoughts to go beyond the situation
iching.hex.52.judgment: Stillness of the mountain — the sage quiets the mind and does not go beyond his position
iching.hex.52.name: Keeping Still
iching.hex.53.image: A tree on the mountain grows slowly — the sage abides in dignity and virtue
iching.hex.53.judgment: Gradual development like a tree on the mountain brings lasting results
iching.hex.53.name: Gradual Progress
iching.hex.54.image: Thunder over the lake — the sage understands the transitory in light of eternity
iching.hex.54.judgment: The younger sister follows the elder — knowing one's place brings eventual good
iching.hex.54.name: The Marrying Maiden
iching.hex.55.image: Thunder and lightning together — the sage decides lawsuits and carries out punishments
iching.hex.55.judgment: Fullness and abundance at the zenith — act now for the sun must set
iching.hex.55.name: Abundance
iching.hex.56.image: Fire on the mountain — the sage is clear and cautious in applying penalties
iching.hex.56.judgment: The traveler succeeds through smallness and adaptability, not grandeur
iching.hex.56.name: The Wanderer
iching.hex.57.image: Winds following one another — the sage spreads his commands and carries out his affairs
iching.hex.57.judgment: Persistent gentle penetration achieves what aggression cannot
iching.hex.57.name: The Gentle
iching.hex.58.image: Lakes joined together — the sage joins with friends for discussion and practice
iching.hex.58.judgment: True joy comes from inner firmness expressed with outward gentleness
iching.hex.58.name: The Joyous
iching.hex.59.image: Wind drives over the water — the ancient kings made offerings and built temples
iching.hex.59.judgment: Wind over water disperses rigid separations — use gentle means to dissolve barriers
iching.hex.59.name: Dispersion
iching.hex.6.image: Heaven and water move apart — the sage plans before acting
iching.hex.6.judgment: Inner truth and caution resolve disagreement
iching.hex.6.name: Conflict
iching.hex.60.image: Water over the lake — the sage creates number and measure to examine virtue and conduct
iching.hex.60.judgment: Proper limits bring structure — but bitter limitations should not be held to
iching.hex.60.name: Limitation
iching.hex.61.image: Wind over the lake — the sage deliberates upon punishments and delays execution
iching.hex.61.judgment: Inner truth reaches even pigs and fish — sincerity moves all beings
iching.hex.61.name: Inner Truth
iching.hex.62.image: Thunder on the mountain — the sage is reverent in conduct and sorrowful in mourning
iching.hex.62.judgment: In small matters excess is permissible — the flying bird leaves its song behind
iching.hex.62.name: Small Excess
iching.hex.63.image: Water over fire — the sage reflects on misfortune and prepares against it
iching.hex.63.judgment: All lines in their proper place — success, but vigilance against disorder
iching.hex.63.name: After Completion
iching.hex.64.image: Fire over water — the sage carefully distinguishes things and puts them in their places
iching.hex.64.judgment: The final crossing is not yet accomplished — careful discernment of forces is needed
iching.hex.64.name: Before Completion
iching.hex.7.image: Water within the earth — the sage nurtures the people and gathers strength
iching.hex.7.judgment: Discipline and just leadership organize the people
iching.hex.7.name: The Army
iching.hex.8.image: Water upon the earth — the ancient kings established their states
iching.hex.8.judgment: Union requires a central figure of sincerity
iching.hex.8.name: Holding Together
iching.hex.9.image: Wind over heaven — the sage refines outward form and virtue
iching.hex.9.judgment: Gentle restraint achieves what force cannot
iching.hex.9.name: Small Taming
islamic.month.1.brief: Sacred month of mourning and remembrance
islamic.month.1.name: محرّم
islamic.month.10.brief: Month of celebration and voluntary fasting
islamic.month.10.event: Eid al-Fitr (1st) -- Festival of Breaking Fast
islamic.month.10.name: شوّال
islamic.month.11.brief: Sacred month of truce and preparation for Hajj
islamic.month.11.name: ذو القعدة
islamic.month.12.brief: Sacred month of pilgrimage and sacrifice
islamic.month.12.event: Hajj (8th-12th), Eid al-Adha (10th) -- Festival of Sacrifice
islamic.month.12.name: ذو الحجة
islamic.month.2.brief: Month of travel -- traditionally considered difficult
islamic.month.2.name: صفر
islamic.month.3.brief: Month of joy and prophetic celebration
islamic.month.3.name: ربيع الأول
islamic.month.4.brief: Quiet month of reflection and growth
islamic.month.4.name: ربيع الآخر
islamic.month.5.name: جمادى الأولى
islamic.month.6.name: جمادى الآخرة
islamic.month.7.name: رجب
islamic.month.8.name: شعبان
islamic.month.9.brief: Month of fasting, revelation, and spiritual renewal
islamic.month.9.name: رمضان
islamic.prayer.0.meaning: Awakening -- beginning before the world stirs
islamic.prayer.0.name: Fajr
islamic.prayer.1.meaning: Remembrance -- pausing at the day's peak
islamic.prayer.1.name: Dhuhr
islamic.prayer.2.name: Asr
islamic.prayer.3.name: Maghrib
islamic.prayer.4.name: Isha
japanese.era.0.brief: The era of enlightened rule — Japan emerged from isolation into the modern age
japanese.era.0.name: Meiji
japanese.era.0.spirit: Rapid modernization, opening to the world, imperial restoration
japanese.era.1.brief: The era of great righteousness — a short season of democracy and artistic freedom
japanese.era.1.name: Taisho
japanese.era.1.spirit: Democratic movement, cultural liberalism, the brief flowering
japanese.era.2.brief: The era of radiant peace — from devastation to the world's second economy
japanese.era.2.name: Showa
japanese.era.2.spirit: War, defeat, rebirth, economic miracle, longest reign
japanese.era.3.brief: The era of achieving peace — prosperity tempered by earthquakes and introspection
japanese.era.3.name: Heisei
japanese.era.3.spirit: The lost decades, natural disasters, quiet resilience
japanese.era.4.brief: The era of beautiful harmony — named from the oldest Japanese poetry anthology
japanese.era.4.name: Reiwa
japanese.era.4.spirit: New era, connection to Man'yoshu poetry, hope and harmony
japanese.rokuyo.0.brief: Win first — seize the morning, the early hours favor bold action
japanese.rokuyo.0.guidance: Morning is auspicious, afternoon unlucky — act early, avoid delay
japanese.rokuyo.0.name: Sensho
japanese.rokuyo.1.brief: Draw friend — what happens to you today may happen to those close to you
japanese.rokuyo.1.guidance: Midday is unlucky, morning and evening good — avoid funerals (the dead draw friends)
japanese.rokuyo.1.name: Tomobiki
japanese.rokuyo.2.brief: Lose first — the day begins poorly but reverses; persistence wins
japanese.rokuyo.2.guidance: Morning is unlucky, afternoon improves — patience is rewarded, wait for the turn
japanese.rokuyo.2.name: Senbu
